political.s: Sally Beauty made minimal political contributions ($4,355 in 2024) with no federal lobbying activity. The company signed the 'Time to Vote' initiative to support voter participation, indicating some non-partisan civic engagement.

charity.s: Sally Beauty launched SBH Inspires foundation in 2023 with $200K initial funding focused on domestic violence support. Historically donated over $150K to domestic abuse shelters and $1.3M to breast cancer research. Current efforts emphasize supporting individuals impacted by domestic violence.
charity.focus: Domestic violence prevention, breast cancer research, community support

environment.s: Sally Beauty implemented 'SBH Going Green' program eliminating plastic bags, retrofitting stores with LED lighting, and establishing energy management systems reducing store consumption by 34%. All owned-brand products are cruelty-free and approximately 95% vegan. The company has retrofit 1,650+ stores and maintains sustainability commitments across operations.

labor.s: Sally Beauty employs approximately 30,000 associates globally. A 2018 lawsuit alleged off-the-clock work for bank deposits, claiming wage and record-keeping violations. Company emphasizes employee safety, health, and has enhanced anti-discrimination policies. Employee compensation ratings remain low (2.7/5 on Glassdoor) with mixed satisfaction regarding benefits.

dei.s: Sally Beauty demonstrated pro-DEI commitment by continuing 'Rooted in Success' initiative to amplify textured haircare leaders and expanding Black-owned brand offerings to 50+. Company established Diversity and Inclusion Council, employee resource groups, and mandatory DEI training. Leadership publicly stated commitment to DEI as business necessity despite industry headwinds.

animals.s: Sally Beauty's owned-brand products are cruelty-free and approximately 95% vegan, with all finished formulas not tested on animals. However, external sources list Sally Beauty Holdings as testing on animals for products sold in certain markets, creating conflicting information about third-party and regional testing practices.

guns.s: Sally Beauty is a beauty supply retailer with no involvement in firearms sales or manufacturing.

privacy.s: Sally Beauty maintains privacy policies for website, retail, and applicant data collection with regional compliance (CCPA, GDPR). The company collects extensive personal information including browsing history, geolocation, and biometric data. Stated practices include data sharing with third-party service providers, though safeguards are mentioned. Overall approach balances compliance with broad data collection.

execPay.s: Sally Beauty CEO Denise Paulonis earned $9.1 million in 2025 (21% increase) despite 0.4% sales decline and missed Adjusted Operating Income targets. Other executives saw increases of 13-31%. CEO compensation is heavily weighted toward variable components (86%) and long-term incentives, though financial performance did not support increases, raising concerns about pay-to-performance alignment.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
